metadata.greeting: Get ready for an exciting weekend in London! I've found lots of fantastic activities perfect for a 3-year-old, from exploring the depths of the ocean at the Horniman Museum to engaging in imaginative play at the Southbank Centre. There are also plenty of free museum options!

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a relaxed and engaging environment for toddlers to explore books and stories, fostering a love for reading.

transport.fromHackney: Bus routes and Overground to Hackney Downs
transport.tip: Check term times before visiting.

activities.title: Interactive Storytelling at The Woodman
activities.description: Interactive storytelling sessions for young children.
activities.whyItWorks: Engaging storytelling format keeps toddlers entertained and encourages participation.

transport.tip: Check specific times and book in advance.

activities.title: Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park Exploration
activities.description: Explore the vast outdoor spaces, playgrounds, and family-friendly attractions in the Olympic Park.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers plenty of space for running around, accessible play areas, and interesting sights for toddlers.

transport.tip: Pack a picnic to enjoy by the water.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A wooden playground with a pond theme, designed specifically for toddlers.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed specifically for toddlers, this playground offers a safe and engaging space for play.

transport.tip: Located within the Olympic Park, easy to combine with other activities.

activities.title: Young V&A Museum
activities.description: Museum designed for children with sensory play areas and interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers sensory experiences suitable for toddlers, encouraging interactive play and exploration.

transport.tip: Check for specific toddler-focused events.

activities.title: Museum of the Home
activities.description: Explore historical homes and gardens. Pick up an activity sheet or explorer backpack from the front desk.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a glimpse into different eras of home life, with hands-on activities for kids and a nice enclosed garden.

transport.tip: Check out the enclosed garden area.

activities.title: Free Football Sessions
activities.description: FREE EASTER FOOTBALL SESSIONS AGES 6-16 YEARS OLD - BETHNAL GREEN.
activities.whyItWorks: Good way to get some physical activity

transport.tip: confirm if younger children can participate

activities.title: Victoria Park Activities
activities.description: Two weeks of Easter activities in Victoria Park. East London's most famous park is well known for the adult festivals it hosts in the summer, but over Easter it practically has its own festival for little ones, with a full fortnight of free, daily, mostly no booking required activities that stretch from sports and fishing (!) on Tuesday March 31 through to pond dipping on Friday April 10.
activities.whyItWorks: Pond dipping is very hands on and engaging for toddlers.

transport.tip: Check the website and the Victoria Park Facebook page.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaur fossils, interactive exhibits, and the history of planet Earth.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers interactive exhibits that are engaging for toddlers, especially the dinosaur exhibits.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Liverpool Street, then Circle/District Line to South Kensington
transport.tip: Visit the dinosaur gallery early to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Hands-on exhibits across seven zones, including the Space gallery.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers interactive exhibits perfect for curious toddlers, allowing them to discover how the world works.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Liverpool Street, then Circle/District Line to South Kensington
transport.tip: Check out the Wonderlab for hands-on experiences (may require separate tickets).

activities.title: Tate Modern
activities.description: Modern and contemporary art with interactive exhibits and space to run free in Turbine Hall.
activities.whyItWorks: The Turbine Hall offers a large open space for toddlers to move around, and the art can spark their curiosity.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then Jubilee Line to Southwark
transport.tip: Check for family-friendly workshops and activities.

activities.title: British Museum
activities.description: Explore world art and artifacts, including Egyptian mummies and the Rosetta Stone.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a cultural experience with interesting artifacts to capture the attention of curious minds.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Tottenham Court Road
transport.tip: Pick up a family activity sheet at the family desk.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: Small zoo in Hampstead Heath with rare birds and animals, including wallabies and lemurs.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a free and accessible opportunity to see animals, which is always a hit with toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Gospel Oak, then Overground to Golders Green
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to Hampstead Heath.

activities.title: Coram's Fields
activities.description: Seven acres dedicated to children, with an adventure playground, sand pits, a paddling pool (summer), and a small city farm.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a wide range of activities for toddlers, including a playground, water play (in summer), and animal encounters.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Russell Square
transport.tip: Adults are only allowed entry if accompanied by a child.

activities.title: Greenwich Park
activities.description: The oldest enclosed Royal Park in London with wide-open spaces, a deer park, and playgrounds.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers ample space for toddlers to run around, a deer park for animal spotting, and playgrounds for active play.

transport.tip: Visit the Royal Observatory and stand on the Meridian Line.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: Family-friendly museum and gardens with a collection of objects, aquarium, and animal enclosures.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a variety of attractions, including an aquarium and gardens, making it a stimulating and engaging day out for toddlers.

transport.tip: Explore the Nature Trail and Butterfly House.

activities.title: Laaaunch!
activities.description: Multi-sensory space-themed show.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed for babies, with multi-sensory elements perfect for engaging little ones

transport.tip: Check times and book in advance

activities.title: Hamley's Easter Happenings
activities.description: Lots of fun Easter happenings at London's biggest toy shop including Chef Chocco's Chocolatiers Puppet Show, Hamley's Rainbow ribbon parade, Storytime, Hamley's Dragon Easter egg hunt… and more!
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a variety of Easter-themed activities and entertainment for children

transport.tip: Check times and book in advance

activities.title: Easter Eggsplorers at Hobbledown Heath
activities.description: Family Easter adventure in West London. Follow the themed egg trail, climb and explore across indoor and outdoor play, and meet animals along the way.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines an Easter egg hunt with animal encounters and play, keeping kids entertained for hours.

transport.tip: Allow a full day to explore everything.

activities.title: Easter at Kew Gardens
activities.description: Learning more about the vital role that animals and plants play in the natural world.
activities.whyItWorks: There are many activities available for kids during the Easter break. Especially in the parks and gardens in London where there are always plenty of events and activities organised for little ones. You'll find games like egg and spoon races, workshops to paint Easter eggs, and the famous Easter egg hunts, where kids have to search for hidden chocolate eggs.

transport.tip: Book tickets in advance

activities.title: Easter at London Zoo
activities.description: Embark on a Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo: Wander past giraffes, lions, monkeys and more incredible wildlife as you search for hidden eggs on a special Easter egg hunt (included in zoo entry).
activities.whyItWorks: A special Easter egg hunt (included in zoo entry)

transport.fromHackney: Train and Tube to Regents Park
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance

activities.title: Easter at Battersea Park Children's Zoo
activities.description: There are many activities available for kids during the Easter break.
activities.whyItWorks: Especially in the parks and gardens in London where there are always plenty of events and activities organised for little ones. You'll find games like egg and spoon races, workshops to paint Easter eggs, and the famous Easter egg hunts, where kids have to search for hidden chocolate eggs.

transport.fromHackney: Train and Tube to Battersea Park
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance

activities.title: Lindt GOLD BUNNY Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Join in the fun as you hunt for the coveted Lindt GOLD BUNNY statues while exploring Henry VIII's gardens and historic surrounds. Once you have found all of the statues, you will be rewarded with a 10g GOLD BUNNY.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed for ages 3-12, it's a perfect day out for the family.

transport.fromHackney: Train and Tube to Hampton Court
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance

activities.title: Spring Craft and Plant Fair at Eltham Palace
activities.description: Explore our medieval Great Hall, where local artisans and makers will showcase jewellery, pottery, artwork and more. Outside the Art Deco mansion, plant stallholders will offer a selection of indoor and outdoor plants, garden accessories, and expert advice.
activities.whyItWorks: offers shopping and crafts

transport.tip: Book tickets in advance
